title: Installation Guide for v1.0
content: This guide details the steps to install version 1.0 of our software on Linux, Windows, and macOS. Ensure you have Python 3.8+ installed.

title: Troubleshooting Network Issues
content: Common network issues include firewall blocks, incorrect IP addresses, and DNS resolution failures. Check your network settings and logs.

title: API Reference: User Authentication
content: The /auth endpoint allows users to log in and receive an access token. Requires username and password in JSON body.

title: Getting Started with Data Processing
content: Begin your journey with data processing by understanding data loading, cleaning, and basic transformation techniques using Pandas.

title: Configuration File Syntax
content: Configuration files use YAML format. Key-value pairs define application behavior. Refer to schema for valid options.

title: Tutorial: Building Your First App
content: Follow this tutorial to create a simple 'Hello World' application using our framework. It covers project setup and basic routing.

title: Database Connection Setup
content: To connect to the database, update the `database.ini` file with your host, port, username, and password. Supports PostgreSQL and MySQL.

title: Error Codes Explained
content: Error code 401 indicates unauthorized access. Code 500 is an internal server error. Consult the full list for details.

title: API Reference: Data Retrieval
content: Use the /data endpoint to retrieve user-specific data. Supports filtering by date range and user ID. Authentication required.

title: Quick Start for Developers
content: New developers can quickly get started by cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and running the local server.

title: Advanced Configuration Options
content: Explore advanced configuration options like caching, load balancing, and environment-specific settings in the `config_advanced.yaml`.

title: Deployment Checklist
content: Before deploying to production, ensure all tests pass, environment variables are set, and monitoring is configured.

title: Security Best Practices
content: Implement strong password policies, use HTTPS, and regularly update dependencies to maintain application security.

title: Performance Tuning Tips
content: Optimize database queries, use caching mechanisms, and scale horizontally to improve application performance.

title: API Reference: Error Handling
content: API error responses follow a standard JSON format with `code`, `message`, and `details` fields for easier debugging.

title: User Interface Customization
content: Customize the look and feel of your application by modifying CSS files and theme settings in the `ui` directory.

title: Logging and Monitoring
content: Set up logging to capture application events and errors. Use monitoring tools to track performance metrics and alerts.

title: Backup and Restore Procedures
content: Regularly back up your database and application files. Test restore procedures to ensure data integrity.

title: Command Line Interface Usage
content: Our CLI tool provides commands for managing projects, deploying, and interacting with the API. Run `mycli --help` for options.

title: Integrating with Third-Party Services
content: Learn how to integrate our platform with popular third-party services like Slack, GitHub, and payment gateways.

title: Version Control with Git
content: Understand basic Git commands: `clone`, `add`, `commit`, `push`, `pull`, and `branch` for collaborative development.

title: Testing Your Application
content: Write unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests to ensure the quality and reliability of your code.

title: Debugging Common Problems
content: Common debugging problems include incorrect variable values, infinite loops, and unhandled exceptions. Use a debugger.

title: Scalability Considerations
content: Design your application for scalability by using stateless components, message queues, and horizontal scaling strategies.

title: Release Notes for v1.1
content: This release introduces new features like dark mode, improved search, and bug fixes for reported issues in v1.0.
